meetings.title: Reading the shape of RNA with LLMs: a frozen RNA language model + probe head that leads on every length tier
meetings.summary: Presented by Vedanth Murari (undergraduate researcher). A pipeline that predicts RNA secondary structure by putting a small trained probe head on a frozen ~100M-parameter DGRNA language model, with a block-tiled head for long-range pairs and a nested (non-crossing) decoder. It matches or beats SuperFold, EternaFold, and RiNALMo on every length tier (pooled base-pair micro-F1 0.898) and recovers 84% of long-range pairs that a conventional band head scores at 0%. Includes the honest failure of a per-family LoRA unfreeze — a same-provenance +0.21 that a pre-registered test showed was memorization, not learning.

meetings.title: MAGF, honestly: why the two-stage decoder plateaued — and the SDF re-engineering that fixes it
meetings.summary: Final results from the optimized 2D two-stage MAGF decoder block. The idea (membrane-aware geometry feedback for organelle segmentation) is promising, but the soft-bias implementation turned out causally inert. The failure is diagnostic, not fatal: an oracle membrane sign-gate drives the topological 'leak' to zero at no cost to real organelles — which points to re-engineering MAGF with learned membrane signed-distance fields (SDFs) so containment is licensed by construction. Includes an SDF primer + where this stands vs the recent CVPR 2026 SDF-for-cryo paper.
